HiveKeeper AI
Hello! I'm your HiveKeeper AI assistant. Ask me anything about beekeeping — inspections, treatments, swarm management, or specific issues with your hives.
What should I look for in April?
How do I check for varroa?
When is swarm season?
Signs of a queenless hive?
How do I treat wax moth?
What's the best winter feed?

Inspection guide
Good conditions to inspect
Above 14°C · No rain · Wind below 15mph · Between 10am and 4pm
How often to inspect
April–August: every 7–10 days · September–October: every 2–3 weeks · November–March: only when above 10°C
Do not inspect when
Below 10°C · Rain falling · Wind above 20mph · After 4pm · Within 48 hours of applying treatment

Share apiary

Invite someone
Pro: up to 3 collaborators per apiary. Invites are sent by email from [email protected].
📋 My sighting reports
✕
Your previously submitted sightings. All reports have been forwarded to Defra/NBU. Tap "View map" to see the exact location.
🚨 Report Asian Hornet
✕
Before reporting: take a clear photo from a safe distance. The CEH/Defra team needs the photo to confirm species. This form sends your report directly to alertnonnative@ceh.ac.uk via HiveKeeper.
Report forwarded via HiveKeeper to alertnonnative@ceh.ac.uk.
NBU typically responds within 24 hours.
🌾 Log a spray
✕
Log pesticide or crop spraying near your apiaries. Helps track exposure risk and informs inspection decisions.
💡 Tip: The UK's free BeeConnected service sends automatic alerts when farmers plan to spray near your hives. Sign up.
🐝 HiveKeeper guide
✕
Getting started
Tap Apiary on the side rail to add your first hive. Give it a name, set its type (National, Langstroth, WBC etc), status, and number of brood boxes and supers. Drag hives to arrange them on your apiary map.
Recording an inspection
Tap Inspect on the side rail. Select your hive, then tap the 🎤 microphone and speak naturally — "queen seen on frame four, six frames of brood, stores adequate, no swarm cells". The AI extracts queen status, brood frames, stores, swarm risk, temper and health score automatically. You can also fill in the form manually.
Home screen
Shows your apiary summary (active hives, lost colonies, unclear status), priority alerts, live weather for your apiary location, seasonal tip, Asian Hornet Watch, and your active hives scroll. Tap the apiary name to switch between multiple apiaries.
Hive detail
Tap any hive in the Apiary screen to open its detail page. Tabs show: inspection timeline, brood nest chart, frame tracker, varroa counts, harvests, and treatments. Tap any inspection to edit it. Queen heritage tracks breeding lineage.
Alerts
The alert engine automatically flags: swarm risk, overdue inspections, low stores, queen issues, varroa levels, Asian hornet risk periods, and seasonal actions. Alerts appear on the home screen ranked by severity.
Varroa monitoring
Log natural mite drop counts and wash counts in the Varroa tab on any hive. Treatment-free beekeepers can use the visual assessment option — mite levels on bees, uncapping behaviour, and exoskeleton damage indicators.
Hiding hives
Tap 👁 Hide on any hive to remove it from the active view without deleting it — useful for equipment being cleaned or colonies on loan. Hidden hives don't count toward your hive limit. Tap 👁 Show hidden in the Apiary screen to reveal and unhide them.
Multiple apiaries
Tap the apiary name on the home screen to add a second location. Each apiary has its own hives, map layout and inspection history. Switch between them with a single tap.
QR & NFC labels
Open any hive and tap the QR code icon to generate a scannable label. Attach it to your hive — scanning it opens straight to that hive's inspection screen.
Calendar
The calendar tab shows inspection history, a 5-day weather forecast, and seasonal tips for the current month — swarm prevention, feeding, treatment windows, and harvest timing.
SOS emergency
The red SOS button sends a pre-written SMS with your GPS location to a saved emergency contact. Set your contact first in ⚙️ Settings → Set emergency contact.
Marketplace
Browse and list bees, equipment and honey for sale. Listings marked HiveKeeper Verified include live hive health data from the seller's inspection records.
Exporting data
In ⚙️ Settings: Export all data to CSV downloads everything. Export to BeeBase/NBU format produces the exact column structure needed for Regional Bee Inspector compliance reporting.

Change hive status
✕

Select the current status of this hive.

📦
Empty — ready to receive bees
Box cleaned and prepared. Waiting for a swarm, nuc, or overwintered colony.
🐝
New nucleus (nuc)
5-frame nuc introduced. Colony building up.
🌀
Prime swarm caught
Large swarm with old queen. Strong start — needs drawn comb or foundation.
🌀
Cast swarm caught
Smaller swarm with virgin queen. Will take longer to build up.
❄️
Overwintered colony moved in
Established colony transferred from another apiary or box.
✂️
Split / artificial swarm
Colony created by splitting an existing hive. Queen cells or virgin queen present.
💀
Colony lost
Mark as lost. Hive will show on map as grey. Equipment needs cleaning.
❓
Unclear — needs investigation
Activity uncertain. Robbing, stuck super, or difficult to inspect.
👑
Mating hive
Small colony used for queen mating. Honey production alerts suppressed.
🏭
Nuc brood factory
Colony managed for brood and nuc production, not honey. Harvest prompts suppressed.
QR & NFC hive tags
✕

Stick a tag on each hive box. Scan it and HiveKeeper opens straight to that hive — no scrolling, no searching.

📱 NFC tags (recommended) Buy NTAG213 NFC stickers online — around 10p each. Use a free NFC writer app (NFC Tools on Android). Write the hive URL to the sticker. Stick inside the roof or on the side. Tap your phone to open straight to the hive.

Move to

You can fine-tune on the map after creating.
Defaults to now — change if the move happened earlier.
🔆 Screen stays awake
✕

While you're on the Inspect screen, HiveKeeper keeps your phone awake. You can leave the screen on in the apiary and get back to inspecting with bee-goo'd gloves without your phone locking you out mid-record.

The indicator

Look for the 🔆 Stay awake marker next to the Online pill. When it's showing, your screen is held on. When you leave Inspect (Home, Apiary, anywhere else) the phone goes back to normal auto-lock. Tap the marker any time to reopen this explanation.

⚠ Battery note

Navigate away from Inspect when you're done — Apiary, Hive, or Home will all release it automatically. Otherwise your battery keeps draining.

Android battery saver mode can block wake lock. If the 🔆 indicator doesn't appear, check your device's power-saving settings.

🌐 Also good to know

If you're connected to a VPN, voice transcription may fail — VPNs can block or reroute the traffic HiveKeeper needs. Disconnect your VPN while recording, or type notes manually instead.

Before you start
Please read and agree to our policies. We've kept them clear and honest.
What we collect: Your apiary data, inspection records and photos. Your email for login.
What we don't: No payment cards, no tracking, no ad networks.
Your data is yours: Export or delete it any time from Settings.
Please tick all three boxes to continue.
You must agree to all three to use HiveKeeper · Version 1.0 · 2 April 2026

Emergency contact
If you tap SOS at the apiary, a message with your GPS location is sent instantly — no confirm step.
🍯
You're all set!
Your apiary is ready. Add more hives from the Apiary tab, record your first inspection from the Inspect tab, and tap a hive on the map to see its detail.
🔍 Bee Inspector Contacts
✕
National Bee Unit (NBU)
📧 beehealth@apha.gov.uk
📞 0300 303 0094
🌐 nationalbeeunit.com
Find Your Regional Inspector
Your Regional Bee Inspector (RBI) is your first point of contact for disease, notifiable conditions, and compliance. Find yours on BeeBase using your postcode.
🚨 Notifiable Diseases — Report Immediately
• American Foulbrood (AFB)
• European Foulbrood (EFB)
• Small Hive Beetle
• Tropilaelaps mite
• Asian Hornet (Vespa velutina)
Do not move equipment if you suspect a notifiable disease. Contact the NBU immediately.
BBKA — British Beekeepers Association
📞 024 7669 6679
🌐 bbka.org.uk
